title: callionia simplex
intro: Callionia simplex, also known as Mexican poppy or calliopsis, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its delicate, pale yellow flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Callionia simplex include its ability to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Callionia simplex is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can be used externally as a poultice or salve to treat various health conditions.
